text: Hello and welcome to Real Easy English.
zh: 大家好，欢迎收听《Real Easy English》。

text: In this podcast we have real conversations in Easy English to help you learn. I'm Beth. And I'm Georgie.
zh: 在这个播客里，我们用简单的英语进行真实对话，帮助大家学习。我是Beth。我是Georgie。

text: You can find a video version of this podcast and a worksheet to help you learn on our website bbclearningenglish .com.
zh: 你可以在我们的网站bbclearningenglish.com上找到这期播客的视频版本和练习题，帮助你学习。

text: Hello, Georgie. How are you today? I'm very well, thank you, Beth.
zh: 你好，Georgie，今天怎么样？我很好，谢谢，Beth。

text: How are you? I'm very good. I'm very good. Today, we are talking about mess.
zh: 你呢？我也很好，我也很好。今天我们要聊聊“乱”这个话题。

text: Now, mess is when something is untidy.
zh: “mess”指的是东西不整洁的状态。

text: I left my house in a bit of a mess this morning.
zh: 我今天早上出门时家里有点乱。

text: Oh dear, Beth, that's not very good. I slept in a bit too long.
zh: 哎呀，Beth，这可不太好。我今天早上睡过头了。

text: My kitchen is a mess. There are pans on the side.
zh: 我的厨房一团糟，锅都堆在一边。

text: I don't want to think about it really. Beth, that sounds disgusting. Are you a messy person in general?
zh: 我都不想去想它了。Beth，听起来真恶心。你平时是个邋遢的人吗？

text: I like things to be clean, but if there is a bit of mess, I'm okay with it.
zh: 我喜欢东西干净整洁，但如果有点乱，我也能接受。

text: But but I try to sort out the mess as soon as I can.
zh: 不过我会尽快把乱糟糟的地方收拾好。

text: But you know how sometimes you cook dinner,
zh: 但你知道吗，有时候你做完晚饭，

text: for example, and then I don't do the washing up immediately because I want to eat my food hot.
zh: 比如说吧，我不会马上洗碗，因为我想趁热吃饭。

text: So I will eat, relax for a bit, and then I will clean the kitchen.
zh: 所以我会先吃饭，休息一下，然后再收拾厨房。

text: Do you have like a rule in your house for the length of time you can leave the washing up
zh: 你家里有没有规定，洗碗最多能拖多久，

text: like does it have to be that evening or can it be the next day
zh: 比如必须当晚洗完，还是可以留到第二天？

text: I like it to be that evening but it's not always that evening which I don't like
zh: 我希望是当晚洗完，但并不总是这样，这点我不太喜欢。

text: what about you in your house is it tidy is messy
zh: 那你呢，你家是整洁还是凌乱？

text: it's usually quite tidy because I live with two other people so
zh: 通常挺整洁的，因为我和另外两个人一起住，

text: we all try to be considerate with the shared spaces so
zh: 我们都尽量为共用空间考虑，

text: yeah and my room I try to tidy every few days so yeah.
zh: 我自己的房间我每隔几天就会整理一次，所以还好。

text: That sounds quite tidy. Beth do you have a favourite job to do around the house or a least favourite job?
zh: 听起来挺整洁的。Beth，你有没有特别喜欢或特别不喜欢做的家务？

text: I have a least favourite job and that is washing up.
zh: 我有一个最不喜欢的家务，那就是洗碗。

text: I hate washing the dishes but we have a dishwasher so i use the dishwasher but
zh: 我讨厌洗碗，不过我们家有洗碗机，所以我基本用洗碗机，

text: there are some things like some pans that i should wash up so
zh: 但有些东西，比如某些锅，还是得手洗，

text: i sometimes do them but my husband does them quite a lot
zh: 所以我有时候会洗，但大部分时候是我丈夫洗的。

text: is there any way that you make washing up a more enjoyable activity
zh: 那你有没有什么办法让洗碗变得更有趣一点？

text: there is i put music on i put a song on and i make sure i finished by the end of the song
zh: 有的，我会放音乐，放一首歌，然后争取在歌播完之前洗完碗。

text: and it just makes me happy because I can just sing along and not focus on hating washing up
zh: 这样我就会很开心，因为我可以跟着唱歌，而不是一直想着讨厌洗碗这件事。

text: and it's not even that bad. I quite like tidying my room because I find it quite satisfying
zh: 其实也没那么糟。我还挺喜欢整理房间的，因为这让我很有成就感，

text: because you you end up with a good result at the end
zh: 因为最后你会看到一个不错的结果。

text: you have a tidy space to relax in and spend time in.
zh: 你会拥有一个整洁的空间，可以放松、待着。

text: That's actually on my to -do list at the moment. Later I'm going to go home and tidy my room.
zh: 这正好在我目前的待办清单上。等下我要回家整理房间。

text: Beth, do you have anything on your to -do list? I do need to hoover.
zh: Beth，你的待办清单上有什么事吗？我确实得吸尘了。

text: So hoovering is on my to -do list.
zh: 所以吸尘就在我的待办清单上。

text: A to -do list is a list of jobs that you need to do.
zh: “to-do list”就是你需要完成的事情清单。

text: So I do need to hoover but I'm not sure if I'll do it today. I might do it tomorrow.
zh: 所以我是得吸尘，但我不确定今天会不会做，可能明天再做吧。

text: OK, let's recap the vocabulary we heard during the conversation, starting with mess.
zh: 好了，我们来回顾一下对话里出现的词汇，先从“mess”开始。

text: A mess is when something is untidy or dirty.
zh: “mess”指的是某样东西又乱又脏的状态。

text: For example, this morning I left my kitchen in a bit of a mess.
zh: 比如说，我今天早上离开家时厨房有点乱。

text: We also use the adjective messy to describe a dirty or untidy place.
zh: 我们也用形容词“messy”来形容脏乱的地方。

text: We can also describe a person as messy, which means that they don't clean things and they often make a mess.
zh: 我们还可以用“messy”来形容一个人，意思是他们不怎么打扫，经常把地方弄乱。

text: The opposite of messy is tidy.
zh: “messy”的反义词是“tidy”（整洁）。

text: So a place like your bedroom or Georgie's bedroom can be tidy.
zh: 所以像你的卧室或者Georgie的卧室这样的地方可以是“tidy”的。

text: And you can also be described as a tidy person.
zh: 你也可以被形容为一个“tidy”的人。

text: That's if you usually clean things and don't make a mess.
zh: 也就是说，你通常会把东西收拾干净，不会弄乱。

text: And a to -do list is a list of jobs you need to do.
zh: “to-do list”就是你需要完成的事情清单。

text: Sometimes jobs around the house, like cleaning. That's it for this episode of Real Easy English.
zh: 有时候是家里的一些活儿，比如打扫卫生。这就是本期《Real Easy English》的全部内容。

text: If you've enjoyed this episode about mess
zh: 如果你喜欢这期关于“乱”的节目，

text: and want to learn more vocabulary for cleaning, Georgie has made a video, Phrasal Verbs for Housework.
zh: 并且想学更多关于打扫卫生的词汇，Georgie制作了一个视频，叫《家务相关的短语动词》。

text: Find it on our website, bbclearningenglish .com.
zh: 可以在我们的网站bbclearningenglish.com上找到。

text: And don't forget to try the worksheet on our website to test what you've
zh: 别忘了在我们网站上试做练习题，测试一下你学到的内容。

text: learnt. We'll be back next week with another conversation in Easy English. See you then. Bye!
zh: 我们下周再见，带来另一段简单英语对话。到时候见，拜拜！
